content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Implemented the remaining prompt-contract amendments for bead `ralph-burning-9ni.6.1`. I tightened bead-description scope parsing so plain colon labels like `Examples:` and `Notes:` no longer break out of embedded `Non-goals` or `Acceptance Criteria` sections and leak work back into `Must-Do Scope`. I rewrote canonical prompt validation to scan section headings sequentially outside fenced code blocks, which preserves rejection of real out-of-order/duplicate canonical sections while allowing verbatim AGENTS guidance and fenced examples to contain canonical heading text. I also added canonical-contract validation before bead-backed project creation persists a prompt override or generated prompt, and restored the repo-root `.ralph-burning/workspace.toml` scaffold so repo-root config loading works again from this checkout.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Patch bead-description scope extraction so generic colon labels inside embedded `Non-goals` and `Acceptance Criteria` remain in those sections instead of re-entering `Must-Do Scope`.
2. [Completed] Update canonical prompt validation to ignore fenced-code headings and verbatim AGENTS guidance collisions while still rejecting real out-of-order canonical sections, and validate canonical prompts before bead-backed project creation persists them.
3. [Completed] Restore the repo-root `.ralph-burning/workspace.toml` scaffold and verify the prompt-contract regression suite plus repo-root config loading behavior.

## Validation Evidence

- Ran `cargo fmt --all` successfully.
- Ran `cargo fmt --check` successfully.
- Ran `cargo test task_prompt_contract -- --nocapture` and the canonical contract validation/guidance tests passed, including the new AGENTS-guidance and fenced-example coverage.
- Ran `cargo test render_bead_task_prompt -- --nocapture` and the prompt-rendering regressions passed, including the new plain-subsection coverage inside embedded `Non-goals` and `Acceptance Criteria`.
- Ran `cargo test create_project_from_bead_context -- --nocapture` and the new invalid-canonical-override guard passed alongside the existing bead bootstrap tests.
- Ran `cargo test rendered_bead_task_prompt_with_embedded_sections_still_satisfies_contract_shape -- --nocapture` and the normalized rendered prompt still satisfied the canonical contract shape.
- Ran `cargo test build_prompt_review_member_prompt -- --nocapture` and prompt-review consumer guidance behavior still passed.
- Ran `cargo run --quiet -- config show` successfully from the repo root after restoring `.ralph-burning/workspace.toml`.

## Outstanding Risks

- Only `.ralph-burning/workspace.toml` was restored as tracked scaffold. The repo still has pre-existing untracked `.ralph-burning/active-project`, `.ralph-burning/projects/`, and `.ralph-burning/prompts/` content, and I did not try to reconstruct a fully tracked runtime workspace from `origin/master`.
- The validator now intentionally ignores canonical heading lines after the final `AGENTS / Repo Guidance` section and inside fenced code blocks. If a future consumer needs duplicate-contract detection inside that final section body, the validator will need a richer markdown-aware parser.
- I ran focused formatting/tests for the prompt-contract path plus a repo-root `config show` check, not the full repository test suite.
